data.title: Nigeria still open to crypto business despite rocky past: Report
data.description:
The government of Nigeria is still open to crypto businesses operating in the country despite the ongoing lawsuit against crypto exchange Binance and the high-profile detention of Binance executive Tigran Gambaryan.
Nigerian Information Minister Mohammed Idris told Semafor that many crypto businesses operate inside the country that are not facing litigation or criminal prosecution.
“This is part of the effort to strengthen our laws, not to cripple anybody. We are ensuring that no one comes and operates without regulation,” Idris told the outlet
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/nigeria-open-crypto-business-despite-rocky-past?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: German regulator rejects Ethena Labs’ license application in the EU, suspects sUSDe is a security
data.description:
Germany’s Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin) has rejected Ethena Labs’ application to issue asset-referenced tokens under the European Union’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCAR). The regulator also raised concerns that the sUSDe may constitute an unlicensed security offering..
source: https://cryptoslate.com/german-regulator-rejects-ethena-labs-license-application-in-the-eu-suspects-susde-is-a-security/

data.title: 🇺🇸 Bitcoin is digital gold and should be treated as such, says Trump’s Executive Director - Council of Advisers for Digital Assets
Coinbase in talks to buy derivatives exchange Deribit: Report
data.description:
Coinbase is in advanced talks to buy Deribit, a cryptocurrency derivatives exchange, according to a March 21 report by Bloomberg.
Acquiring Deribit — the world’s largest venue for trading Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH) options — would bolster Coinbase’s existing derivatives platform, which currently focuses on futures. 
Coinbase and Deribit have reportedly alerted regulators in Dubai to the deal talks. Deribit holds a license in Dubai, which would need to be transferred to Coinbase if a deal goes through, according to Bloomberg, which cited unnamed sources
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/coinbase-talks-to-buy-derivatives-exchange-deribit-report?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Perplexity AI Expresses Interest in Acquiring TikTok's U.S. Operations
data.description: According to BlockBeats, AI search engine company Perplexity AI has announced its intention to acquire TikTok's U.S. operations before a potential government ban takes effect. The company claims it has a unique advantage in restructuring TikTok's algorithm without creating a monopoly. Other potential buyers reportedly interested in TikTok include Oracle, Microsoft, Walmart, former Los Angeles Dodgers owner Frank McCourt, and Roblox CEO Jesse Tinsley. Previously, there were also rumors about Elon Musk considering a purchase.
